textDisplay: I went to this movie last night.  I thought it was going to be a comedy.  It was super over-the-top violent with people getting shot multiple times and impaled.  Who thinks that kind of repetitive violence is---entertainment?  Who is entertained by that?  This is Hollywood at or near its worst.  I should have been tipped off by the January release that it was going to be crap.  I walked out and I am disappointed in Will Smith.  He can do better......

OK people need to seriously stop talking shit about Martin Lawrences weight. Will Smith is 51, Martin Lawrence is 54. I am starting to think the "comics superheroes" stuff have gone to some peoples heads. People will change when they get older. Nothing about Martin Lawrence change makes him out of character. Be happy they finally got to make this movie (Bad Boys 3 have been in production h-ll forever).....Some people would consider this a high risk project based on how Will Smith and Martin Lawrence have been doing lately in other roles. But there is still something particularly enjoyable about them in these particular roles and I am really happy to see this movie is doing good. Specially considering its lack of that "woke poison" we have seen in many other sequels.

You know, I just think it's bizarrely irresponsible/ gross to make a movie celebrating cops who shoot a lot of people. How is this premise in any way positive/alright when we have such a horrendous issue with police violence in this country??? And to make both the cops black???? What? Is it supposed to be funnier, or less offensive that way? I don't think the cops should be shooting anyone. Like, do we really just think cops have/ should have execution powers nowadays? No one thinks it's a human right to a fair trial by a jury of their peers??? This movie looks EXTREMELY STUPID and I'm pretty disappointed that this is still how these two fine actors wanna build their legacy.
Stfu
